name: flow-guard always exits with code zero so it never blocks the operator's PreToolUse flow even when warning fires
command: mkdir -p .claude-tdd-pro/flow-guard && for i in 1 2 3 4 5 6 7 8; do printf '{"tool":"Bash","at":"2026-05-13T00:00:0%dZ"}\n' "$i" >> .claude-tdd-pro/flow-guard/recent.jsonl; done && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/skills/flow-guard/check.sh" --window-min 5 --threshold 5 --now 2026-05-13T00:01:00Z 2>out.txt; rc=$?; [ "$rc" -eq 0 ] && grep -q 'warning' out.txt && echo ok 1>&2
